graded_findings.rationale: The gather finding describes appropriate core LogSampler coverage rather than an adherence gap. There is no merge-blocking test deficiency in this item.
The gather finding reports suitable config validation and retention coverage. It does not identify a missing or weak test obligation.
The gather finding describes targeted access/client/request_out sampling coverage. This is positive evidence, not a substantive tests-updated gap.
The gather finding says the new AccessRecorder facades and sampled-out behavior are covered. No actionable testing deficiency is raised here.
The gather finding identifies integration coverage for logging macros and sampling behavior. This is not a problem finding.
The gather finding says existing macro tests were updated for the sampler semantics. It does not indicate an adherence issue.
The gather finding reports updated RPC metadata assertions for the new sampling states. This is appropriate coverage rather than a gap.
The gather finding describes system coverage for cross-process consistency and config delivery. It is positive evidence, not a merge concern.
The gather finding describes hot-path throughput and lazy construction coverage. That coverage aligns with the performance-sensitive nature of the change.

The gather reports that sampler-specific Flags::UpdateFlagParameter transaction, rollback, and explicit-state behavior changed without assertions in the touched flags test. That is a real regression-test gap for shared configuration behavior and should stop merge until covered or justified.
